text.title: go-layout-directory-structure Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for go-layout-directory-structure as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: pkg/ cargo-cult: adding pkg/ from the layout template without anything to publish, so it becomes a second random bin of utilities.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Skip pkg/ until you have a real external API. Empty or utility-only pkg/ trees signal layout-as-ritual rather than layout-as-architecture.

failure-mode.description: internal/ tree too deep: internal/service/order/v2/usecases/... defeats the navigability the layout was supposed to bring.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Depth beyond three levels negates the benefit of a flat layout; flatten or use package-by-feature inside internal/ instead.

failure-mode.description: Config sprawl: internal/config becomes a god struct that depends on every package's options.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Each package should own its own config struct; internal/config should compose them, not import them all directly.

failure-mode.description: Migrations + binary coupling: migrations/ lives at the root but there is no cmd/migrate, so the dance of applying migrations is undiscoverable.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Without cmd/migrate and a Makefile target, every developer invents a different apply command; this silently skips migrations in CI.
